name: mcp-integration
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "add MCP server", "integrate MCP", "configure MCP in plugin", "use .mcp.json", "set up Model Context Protocol", "connect external service", mentions "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} with MCP", or discusses MCP server types (SSE, stdio, HTTP, WebSocket). Provides comprehensive guidance for integrating Model Context Protocol servers into Claude Code plugins for external tool and service integration.
capabilities: Configure MCP servers using .mcp.json or inline plugin.json settings
Set up stdio servers for local process communication
Connect SSE servers with OAuth authentication
Configure HTTP and WebSocket servers with token authentication
Use environment variables for secure credential management
Pre-allow MCP tools in command frontmatter for security
author: anthropics
securitySummary: This is an official Anthropic documentation skill teaching MCP server integration. All static findings are false positives - code examples in documentation files (not executable code) showing legitimate patterns for building MCP servers. The skill explicitly teaches security best practices including using environment variables for secrets and HTTPS for connections.
valueStatement: Connect Claude Code plugins to external services and APIs using Model Context Protocol. This skill provides configuration patterns for stdio, SSE, HTTP, and WebSocket server types with authentication support.
userTitle: Integrate MCP servers into Claude Code plugins
limitations: Does not create MCP servers - only teaches configuration patterns
OAuth flow managed by Claude Code, not configurable by skill
Does not handle server implementation or SDK usage
Environment variables must be set by user before plugin use

useCases.title: Add external service access
useCases.description: Integrate databases, APIs, and file systems into plugins using MCP server configuration.

useCases.title: Connect cloud services
useCases.description: Configure hosted MCP servers with OAuth for accessing cloud APIs and services.

useCases.title: Bundle custom tools
useCases.description: Package local MCP servers with plugins for automatic tool discovery and usage.

promptTemplates.title: Add MCP server
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I add an MCP server to my plugin that connects to a local database using stdio transport?

promptTemplates.title: OAuth configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure an SSE MCP server for a hosted service that uses OAuth authentication. Show me the .mcp.json format.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-server setup
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I configure multiple MCP servers in a single plugin for different service types?

promptTemplates.title: Environment variables
promptTemplates.prompt: What is the best way to handle API keys and database URLs in MCP server configuration without hardcoding them?

bestPractices: Always use ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} for file paths to ensure portability across installations
Store credentials in environment variables using ${VAR_NAME} syntax, never hardcode secrets
Pre-allow specific MCP tools in command frontmatter using full tool names, avoid wildcard patterns
antiPatterns: Hardcoding absolute paths instead of using ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} for portability
Committing credentials or tokens to version control in configuration files
Using HTTP instead of HTTPS for MCP server connections, exposing credentials in transit

faq.question: What is Model Context Protocol?
faq.answer: MCP is a protocol that enables Claude Code plugins to integrate with external services by exposing their capabilities as tools.

faq.question: How do I test MCP servers locally?
faq.answer: Use localhost URLs, run /mcp command to verify connection, and enable --debug mode to troubleshoot.

faq.question: Can I bundle MCP servers with my plugin?
faq.answer: Yes, include server executables in your plugin directory and reference them with ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} paths.

faq.question: How does OAuth work with MCP?
faq.answer: Claude Code handles OAuth automatically for SSE servers, prompting users in browser on first use.

faq.question: Where are MCP tools available?
faq.answer: MCP tools appear with mcp__plugin_<name>__<tool-name> prefix and work in any command with pre-allowance.

faq.question: What happens if the MCP server fails?
faq.answer: Handle connection failures in commands with fallback behavior and clear error messages for users.
